channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: DHS marks 'one of the most violent days' of Operation Midway Blitz with several arrests
item.description: Homeland Security details "one of the most violent days" of Operation Midway Blitz amid President Donald trump's crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Company recalls 2 million pounds of barbecue pork jerky that may contain metal wire
item.description: (The Associated Press) -- A South Dakota company is recalling more than 2.2 million pounds (998,000 kilograms) of Korean barbecue pork jerky sold at Costco and Sam's Club stores because the product may be contaminated with pieces of metal, federal health officials said Friday. LSI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, KREX 5

item.title: How the growing penny shortage may impact you
item.description: Several retailers have started to roll out policies as they run out of pennies. Here's what to expect if you're paying with cash.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?' New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Family matriarch dies day after rescue from remote island that saw family swim ashore to escape boat fire
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard rescued a family from Naushon Island after their boat caught fire, forcing them to swim to safety from Martha's Vineyard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-abortion pregnancy centers are looking to offer much more than ultrasounds and diapers
item.description: More anti-abortion pregnancy centers have been offering medical services such as testing for sexually transmitted infections.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Filipino social media star dies at 19 in her Los Angeles home, family says
item.description: Emman was the daughter of Filipino television presenter Alejandro "Kim" Ilagan Atienza, also known as Kuya Kim, and his wife Felicia Hung Atienza, a celebrity entrepreneur and health and well-being advocate.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: Congresswoman scammed in her home by fake cleaning crew, police say
item.description: Del. Eleanor Holmes Norton (D-D.C.) was scammed on Thursday by a group claiming to be a cleaning crew, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill to fund SNAP in shutdown introduced in House
item.description: Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa) on Friday introduced a bill to fund the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as the government shutdown threatens payments of the food aid in some states.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Reagan ad that infuriated Trump set to run during World Series
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said on Friday that the anti-tariffs advertisement featuring former President Reagan, which angered President Trump, will air during the World Series before being paused on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, WGN 9

item.title: Here's what the White House lost with the East Wing's demolition
item.description: The East Wing's razing was met with criticism both by preservationists and by over half of the American public.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Task force says man to be deported 3rd time after arrest
item.description: The task force said he has an active order of removal from the country and will be deported for the third time.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, is pledging to embrace his Muslim identity amid growing attacks in the final days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: “In some instances, stopping your medicine may be more harmful to your health than continuing to take the recalled medicine,” the FDA says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Louisiana jury awards more than $40 million to family of man who died in privately-run jail
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A federal jury found a private company running a Louisiana jail liable for the 2015 death of a man who died of head injuries he received while in custody, and awarded the family more than $40 million in damages. Attorneys representing Erie Moore Sr.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and R.J. RICO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Entire White House East Wing now demolished as Trump moves forward with ballroom construction
item.description: The entire White House East Wing has been demolished as President Donald Trump moves forward with a ballroom construction, Associated Press photos on Thursday showed.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cracker Barrel CEO explains reason for attempt to change logo: report
item.description: Cracker Barrel had debuted its new logo in August, but quickly faced backlash for ditching a depiction of the “Old Timer” previously seen on the sign.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Medicare agency recalling thousands of furloughed staffers for open enrollment
item.description: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) is temporarily calling back all furloughed staff starting next week, a spokesperson confirmed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: White House releases donor list for Trump ballroom amid East Wing demolition
item.description: Amazon, Apple, Google and other major companies are among those donating to help cover the cost of President Trump's massive new ballroom at the White House. The White House confirmed the list to The Hill amid demolition of the East Wing to make room for the ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: 53 percent disapprove of White House East Wing demolition: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans said in a new poll they disapprove of President Trump’s plans to demolish part of the East Wing in the White House to make room for his $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Trail Blazers’ Billups, Heat’s Rozier among dozens charged in Mafia-linked gambling probe
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier are among more than 30 people who have been charged in connection with schemes involving illegal sports betting and rigged poker games backed by Mafia, authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NYC woman says she lives in ‘constant fear’ after months-long ICE detention
item.description: “I lost three months of my life. I don’t want to [lose] my life again. I live in constant fear of being re-arrested,” Kostak said. “I don’t want to go back there.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, WGN 9

item.title: Man taken into custody after driving car into White House gate: Secret Service
item.description: A man was taken into custody late Tuesday after driving his car into a portion of the White House security gate, a Secret Service official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Chess grandmaster Daniel Naroditsky is remembered as a leader in the game's online surge
item.description: Daniel Naroditsky, a 29-year-old standard-bearer in the world of competitive speed chess that flourished in the COVID-19 pandemic, died over the weekend and leaves behind a legacy as one of the greats of the game who helped usher in its digital era.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawsuit seeks to force swearing in of US Rep.-elect Adelita Grijalva of Arizona
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Arizona Attorney General Kris Mayes has filed a lawsuit that seeks to get Democrat Adelita Grijalva sworn in as the state’s newest member of Congress after U.S. House Speaker Mike Johnson has refused to seat her a month since winning the post.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The women of NXT are the WWE champions of tomorrow
item.description: It's being called the most exciting and diverse group in the WWE, and it's happening every Tuesday night on NXT.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, Fox 5

item.title: Trump-endorsed candidate enters race to challenge Republican Rep. Thomas Massie of Kentucky
item.description: SHELBYVILLE, Ky. (AP) — President Donald Trump's choice to challenge maverick Republican Rep. Thomas Massie of Kentucky announced his candidacy Tuesday, pledging to steadfastly support the president's agenda while describing the incumbent as an obstructionist.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Warner Bros. signals it may be open to buyout offers
item.description: Just months after announcing plans to split into two companies, Warner Bros. Discovery has signaled that it may be open to a sale of its business.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A Grand Teton mountain runner set a record. But his route turned into a crime
item.description: Michelino Sunseri climbed past boulders on his way to a record-breaking feat: The extreme runner reached the summit of Grand Teton, one of the highest mountains in the American West, and then rushed back down, all in less than three hours. The National Park Service, however, didn’t celebrate.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ICE would need more money to expand use of bodycams in Chicago crackdown, official says
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Many federal officers assigned to immigration enforcement in the Chicago area have body cameras but Congress would have to allocate more funds to expand their use, officials testified Monday at a hearing about the tactics agents are using in Trump administration’s crackdown, which has
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The White House starts demolishing part of the East Wing to build Trump's ballroom
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House on Monday started tearing down part of the East Wing, the traditional base of operations for the first lady, to build President Donald Trump's $250 million ballroom despite lacking approval for construction from the federal agency that oversees such projects.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Nicholas Rossi, man accused of faking own death after Utah rape, given 5 years to life
item.description: Nicholas Rossi, the man who was extradited from Scotland to Utah to face 2008 rape allegations, has been sentenced in Salt Lake County to five years to life in prison.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, Fox 5

item.title: Adam Driver on Jarmusch, 'Star Wars' and putting filmmakers first
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Props, mementos and photographs adorn Adam Driver ’s Brooklyn office. There’s an artwork Jim Jarmusch gave him for his 40th birthday, the doll from Leos Carax’s “Annette” and dozens of on-set photographs, including one of Driver and his son in the Millennium Falcon.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, ABC 4
